mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance against zero during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to generate a positive rank IC over 60-day horizons in backtesting.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates during early-cycle recovery phases, signaling capacity utilization improvements that boost corporate profit margins and drive positive market returns.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when applied outside post-recession recovery periods or during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation readings above 2.5% trigger expectations of central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations over the near term.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally following inflation spikes above the threshold, yielding positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer demand, leading to positive equity performance.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative or flat forward returns over 60 days when tested against historical regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 3.0% annually during late-cycle expansions indicates overheating that prompts monetary policy normalization, resulting in negative equity forward returns.
failure_condition: High GDP growth periods consistently yield positive equity returns, contradicting the overheating mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly industrial production declines reflect weakening manufacturing output that reduces corporate earnings expectations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains positive or statistically insignificant during industrial production contractions over 5-day horizons.
